title: Family Time in Tucson and Friend Time in Guatemala
summary:
While Helen was in Guatemala, I spent some time in Tucson with my family. The main objective of the trip was to help get a lot of landscaping done in order to get my parent's yard ready to host a pre-wedding reception for my sister's upcoming wedding in April! My dad and I shoveled multiple tons of landscaping gravel around the yard and built a rolling gate to block the carport. It was very nice to be back home in Tucson without having to work or take vacation time. I realized that I hadn't had a trip to Tucson where I wasn't working remotely since my senior year of college! Despite the hard work, it was a very relaxing trip, and my dad and I spent our free time making dinner and binging the TV show Three Body Problem on Netflix.

-Colin

While Colin was in Tucson, I got to spend a week with four of my girlfriends from Missoula in Guatemala! We mainly chose Guatemala because my friend Tiffany wanted to hike to see Volcan de Fuego, which is an active Volcano right outside the town of Antigua. We did an overnight tour where we hiked a very steep nearby volcano to a basecamp that gave us views of the erupting volcano. It was a a tough hike with lots of dust and a hoard of stray dogs to keep us company but we all made it up and got to watch the volcano erupt all night, the lava illuminating red once the sun went down. It was honestly crazy to watch and listen to. Other than the tour, we spent a couple of days in the cute cobblestoned town of Antigua where we ate lots of tacos, practiced our spanish, toured churches and ruins and got to catch up before we headed to a couple of towns on Lake Atitlan where we got to swim and relax. It was so fun to spend so much time with my friends and explore a new place together. Guatemala is a gorgeous country with great food and people, just be prepared for treacherous transportation like bumpy boat rides and 13 year old shuttle drivers.

-Helen
